question: A river has a steady speed of $v$. A man swims upstream at a distance of $d$ and swims back to the starting point in total time $t$. The man can swim at a speed of $2 v$ in still water. If the time taken by the man in still water is $t_{0}$ to complete the same length of swim, then $t / t_{0}$ is
explanation:
Speed of river $=v$
Speed of man in still water $=2 v$
$\therefore$ Speed upstream $=(2 v-v)$
Speed downstream $=(2 v+v)$
Now, according to the question, total time taken in going upstream and back downstream to the starting point $=t$
$\therefore {t_{up}} + {t_{down}} = t\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
Given the total distance on one way $=d$$t_{u p}=\dfrac{d}{2 v-v} \Rightarrow t_{\text {down }}=\dfrac{d}{2 v+v}$
Putting these values is Eq. (1) we get,$\dfrac{d}{2 v-v}+\dfrac{d}{2 v+v}=t$
$ \Rightarrow d\left[ {\frac{1}{v} + \frac{1}{{3v}}} \right] = t \Rightarrow t = \frac{{4d}}{{3v}}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
It is given that time taken by the man to cover the same distance in still water $=t_{0}$
$\Rightarrow t_{0}=\dfrac{2 d}{2 v}=\dfrac{d}{v}$
$\therefore$ From Eq. (2)$t=\dfrac{4}{3} t_{0} \Rightarrow \dfrac{t}{t_{0}}=\dfrac{4}{3}$
So, the correct option is (4).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains $A$ and $B$ travel on two parallel rail tracks in opposite direction with speed $v_{1}$ and $v_{2}$ respectively. They take $4 s$ to pass each other at this speed. If the speed of train $A$ is increased by $50 \%$, then they take $3 s$ to pass each other. The ratio of $v_{1} / v_{2}$ is
explanation:
Relative velocity of trains $=v_{1}+v_{2}$
Let length of each train be $s$.
(i) First they take $4 s$ to cross
$2s = \left( {{v_1} + {v_2}} \right)4\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
(ii) Second they take $3 s$ to cross, when speed of train $A$ is increased by $50 \%$.
Now, relative velocity
$ = \left( {{v_1} + \frac{{50}}{{100}}{v_1}} \right) + {v_2} = \left( {\frac{3}{2}{v_1} + {v_2}} \right)$
$\therefore 2s = \left( {\frac{3}{2}{v_1} + {v_2}} \right)3\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
From Eqs. (1) and (2), we get
$\begin{aligned}& \left(v_{1}+v_{2}\right) 4=\left(\dfrac{3}{2} v_{1}+v_{2}\right) 3 \\& \Rightarrow v_{2}=\dfrac{v_{1}}{2} \Rightarrow \dfrac{v_{1}}{v_{2}}=\dfrac{2}{1}\end{aligned}$
So, the correct option is (3).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two towns $X$ and $Y$ are connected by a regular bus service. $A$ bus leaves in either direction at every $t=T$ minutes. A man moving with same speed in the direction $X$ to $Y$ find that a bus goes past him every $t=t_{1}$ minutes in the direction of his motion, and every $t=t_{2}$ minutes in the opposite direction. Then, $T$ is given by
explanation:
The given situation is shown below. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764957671/preptracker/questions/q_106155_explanation_1764957671081.png Now, speed of bus in $X$ to $Y$-direction observed by man $=v-u$ and speed of bus in $Y$ to$X$ - direction observed by man $=v+u$
Buses moves in either direction with equal speed $v$ and at equal time intervals $T$.
So, distance between buses coming from either direction is same.
So, speed $\times$ time $=$ speed $\times$ time $(X$ to $Y)(Y$ to $X)$
$\Rightarrow(v-u) t_{1}=(v+u) t_{2}$
$\Rightarrow v\left(t_{1}-t_{2}\right)=u\left(t_{1}+t_{2}\right)$
or $u=\dfrac{v\left(t_{1}-t_{2}\right)}{t_{1}+t_{2}}$
Hence, distance $D$ between any 2 -buses is
$D=(v-u) t_{1}$$=\left(v-\dfrac{v\left(t_{1}-t_{2}\right)}{t_{1}+t_{2}}\right) t_{1}$
$=v\left(\dfrac{t_{1}+t_{2}-t_{1}+t_{2}}{t_{1}+t_{2}}\right) t_{1}$
$D=v\left(\dfrac{2 t_{1} t_{2}}{t_{1}+t_{2}}\right)$
Hence, time interval between any 2 -buses is
$T=\dfrac{\text { Distance }}{\text { Speed }}=\dfrac{D}{v}=\dfrac{2 t_{1} t_{2}}{t_{1}+t_{2}}$
So, the correct option is (1).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two cars $A$ and $B$ initially at rest are moving in same direction with accelerations $a_{1}$ and $a_{2}$ respectively. After a certain time, they achieve velocities $v_{1}$ and $v_{2}$ and separated by a distance of $50\,m$. If $\left(a_{1}-a_{2}\right)=4 m s^{-2}$, then the quantity $\left(v_{1}-v_{2}\right)$ will be
explanation:
Distance between $\operatorname{cars} A$ and $B, s=50 m$
Net acceleration, $a_{\text {net }}=4 {~ms}^{-2}$
By using second equation of motion, $v_{12}^{2}-u_{12}^{2}=2 a_{12} s$
where $v_{12}=v_{1}-v_{2}=$ velocity of 1 relative to 2 and
$a_{12}=a_{1}-a_{2}=$ acceleration of 1 relative to 2.
$\therefore \,\,{\left( {{v_1} - {v_2}} \right)^2} - 0 = 2\left( {{a_1} - {a_2}} \right)s$
$\Rightarrow\left(v_{1}-v_{2}\right)^{2}=2 \times 4 \times 50=400$
$\Rightarrow v_{1}-v_{2}=20 {~ms}^{-1}$
So, the correct option is (2).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question: Two buses $A$ and $B$ are moving in opposite direction. Now if the first bus $A$ moves towards east with a speed of $36\,km/h$ and bus $B$ moves towards west with a speed of $18\,km/h$, then the bus $B$ appears to bus $A$ as
explanation:
The given situation is as shown in figure, https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764957664/preptracker/questions/q_106141_explanation_1764957664422.png Velocity of bus $A$,
${v_A} = 36\,km/h = 36 \times \frac{5}{{18}} = 10\,m/s$
Velocity of bus $B$,
${v_B} = 18\,km/h = 18 \times \frac{5}{{18}} = 5\,m/s$
Let the speed along east is positive.
The relative speed of bus $B$ w.r.t. $A$ is
$v_{B A}=v_{B}-v_{A}=(-5)-(10)=-15 m / s$
Therefore, the bus $B$ appears to bus $A$ as moving with a speed of $15\,m/s$ from east to west.
So, the correct option is (3).

options.text: moving with a speed of $5\,m/s$ from east to west
moving with a speed of $15\,m/s$ from west to east
moving with a speed of $15\,m/s$ from east to west
moving with a speed of $10\,m/s$ from east to west

subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A car travelling at $15\,m/s$ overtake another car travelling at $10\,m/s$. Assuming, each car is $4\,m$ long. What is the time taken during the overtake?
explanation:
The motion of two cars $A$ and $B$ is shown in the figure. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764952029/preptracker/questions/q_106140_explanation_1764952028400.png Relative velocity of car $A$ w.r.t. car $B$ is
${v_{AB}} = {v_A} - {v_B} = 15 - 10 = 5\,m/s$
Distance to be covered for overtake is given as $s=4 m+4 m=8 m$
Time taken by cars to cross $=$ Distance $/$ speed
$ = \frac{s}{{{v_{AB}}}} = \frac{8}{5} = 1.6\,s$
So, the correct option is (1).
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A stone is dropped from a height of $100\,m$, while another one is projected vertically upwards from the ground with a velocity of $25\,m/s$ at the same time. The time in seconds after which they will have the same height is (acceleration due to gravity, $g = 10\,m{s^{ - 2}}$)
explanation:
Initial position of the stones are shown below. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764952024/preptracker/questions/q_106125_explanation_1764952023814.png Let stones achieve the same height after time $t$ seconds. Then distance travelled by first stone in $t$ seconds
$=100-h(m)$ and distance travelled by $I{I^{nd}}$ stone in $t$ second is $h(m)$. So, we have
For first stone, $ - (100 - h) = \frac{{ - 1}}{2}g{t^2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
For second stone, $h = 25t - \frac{1}{2}g{t^2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
Substituting for $\dfrac{1}{2} g t^{2}$ from Eq. (1) in Eq. (2), we get
$h=25 t-(100-h) \Rightarrow 25 t=100 \Rightarrow t=4 s$
So, the correct option is (1)
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Consider a vehicle moving with a velocity $54\,km/h$. At a distance of $400\,m$, from the traffic light brakes are applied. The acceleration of the vehicle, after the application of brakes is $ - 0.3\,m/{s^2}$. The vehicle's position relative to the traffic light is
explanation:
Given, initial velocity,
$u = 54\,km/h = 54 \times \frac{5}{{18}}\,m/s \Rightarrow u = 15\,m/s$
$d = 400\,m$
acceleration, $a = - 0.3\,m/{s^2}$
Now, By using $v^{2}=u^{2}+2 a s$
$0 = {(15)^2} - 2 \times 0.3 \times s \Rightarrow s = \frac{{225}}{{0.6}} = 375\,m$
Relative position $=400-375=25 m$
So, the correct option is (1)
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two cities $X$ and $Y$ are connected by a regular bus service with a bus leaving in either direction every $T \mathrm{~min}$. A girl is driving scooty with a speed of $60\,km/h$ in the direction $X$ to $Y$ notices that a bus goes past her every 30 minutes in the direction of her motion, and every 10 minutes in the opposite direction. Choose the correct option for the period $T$ of the bus service and the speed(assumed constant) of the buses.
explanation:
Let $v$ be the velocity of buses. As buses leave from stations after every $T$ seconds. The distance between any two successive buses is $v T$. The cases are shown in the following figures. https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764952021/preptracker/questions/q_106120_explanation_1764952020812.png It is given that the two buses passes the scooter with a time interval of 30 minutes.
$(v - 60)\left( {\frac{{30}}{{60}}} \right) = vT{\rm{ }}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$ https://res.cloudinary.com/doe6oiv2h/image/upload/v1764952022/preptracker/questions/q_106120_explanation_1764952021585.png The two buses passes the scooter with a time interval of 10 minutes
$(v + 60)\left( {\frac{{10}}{{60}}} \right) = vT{\rm{ }}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
From eq's 1 & 2 we get $V = 120\,km/hr\,\,{\text{and}}\,\,T = 15\,\min $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A train of $150\,m$ in length is going towards the north direction at a speed of ${10 {~ms}^{-1}}$. A parrot flies at a speed of ${5 {~ms}^{-1}}$ towards the south direction parallel to the railway track. The time taken by the parrot to cross the train is equal to
explanation:
Velocity of the train and parrot are in opposite direction $w.r.t.$ ground.
${v_{T P}=v_{T}-v_{P}=10-(-5)=15 {~m} / {s}}$
Time taken ${=\dfrac{\text { Distance }}{\begin{array}{l}\text { Relative velocity of } \\ \text { train w.r.t parrot }\end{array}}}$
${=\dfrac{150}{15} s=10 s}$

question: Two stones are simultaneously thrown at ${t=0}$ towards each other along a straight line, as shown in the diagram. Gravity is acting downwards ${g=10 {~m} / {s}^{2}}$. The time when two stones collide is
explanation:
${V_{B A}=v_{B}-v_{A}=17-(-13)=30 {~m} / {s}}$
So, $w.r.t.$ ${A, B}$ is moving with constant velocity of ${30 {~m} / {s}}$ and distance required (relative) for collision is $60\,m$, so
${t=\dfrac{60}{30}=2 s}$

question: Two bodies 1 and 2 are projected simultaneously with velocities $v_{1}=2 {~ms}^{-1}$ and $v_{2}=$ $4 {~ms}^{-1}$ respectively. The body 1 is projected vertically up from the top of a cliff of height $h=10 {~m}$ and the body 2 is projected vertically up from the bottom of the cliff. If the bodies meet, find the time  of meeting of the bodies.
explanation:
Let the particle meet after a time $t$. First of all, we choose the point of collision above the top of the cliff.
For the first particle, $s=s_{1}, v_{0}=v_{1}, a=-g$
Then, ${s_1} = {v_1}t - \frac{1}{2}g{t^2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
For the second particle, $s=s_{2}, v_{0}=v_{2}, a=-g$
Then, ${s_2} = {v_2}t - \frac{1}{2}g{t^2}\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
Referring to the figure, ${s_2} - {s_1} = h\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,(3)$
Substituting $s_{1}$ from Eq. (1), $s_{2}$ from Eq. (2) in Eq. (3), we have
$t=\dfrac{h}{v_{2}-v_{1}}=\dfrac{10}{4-2}=5 {~s}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question:
A police jeep is chasing a culprit going on a motorbike. The motorbike crosses a turning at a speed of $72{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} km\,{h^{ - 1}}.$ The jeep follows it at a speed of $90{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} km\,{h^{ - 1}},$ crossing the turning 10 $s$ later than the bike.
Assuming that they travel at constant speeds, how far from the turning will be jeep catch up with the bike?
explanation:
${V_p} = 90\,km\,{h^{ - 1}} = 25\,m{s^{ - 1}}$
${V_c} = 72\,km\,{h^{ - 1}} = 20\,m{s^{ - 1}}$
$\ln 10 {~s}$ culprit reaches point $B$ from $A$.
Distance covered by culprit, $S=v t=20 \times 10=200 {~m}$
At time $t=10 {~s}$, the police jeep is 200 m behind the culprit.
Relative velocity between jeep and culprit is$25 - 20 = 5\,m{s^{ - 1}}.$
Time $=\dfrac{S}{V}=\dfrac{200}{5}=40 {~s}$ (Relative velocity is considered)
In 40 $s$ , the police jeep will move from $A$ to a distance $S$. Where $S = vt = 25 \times 40 = 1000\,m = 1.0\,km$ away.
The jeep will catch up with the bike $1\,km$ far from the turning
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A police car $B$ is chasing a thief 's car $A$. Cars $A$ and $B$ are moving at constant speed $v_{1}=30 {~m} / {s}$ and $v_{2}=20 {~m} / {s}$ respectively along a straight line. The police decides to open fire and a policeman starts firing with his machine gun directly aiming at car $A$. The bullets have a velocity $u=210 {~m} / {s}$ relative to the gun. The policeman keeps firing for an interval of $T_{0}=40 {~s}$. The thief experiences that the time gap between the first and the last bullet hitting his car is $\Delta t$. Find $\Delta t$ (in $sec$).
explanation:
Let $x=$ distance between the car at the instant first bullet is fired (say at time $t=0$ )
Velocity of car $A$, relative to car $B, v_{A B}=30-20=10 {~m} / {s}$
Speed of bullet, relative to ground,
$v_{b}=u+v_{2}=210+20=230 {~m} / {s}$
Velocity of bullet, relative to car $A,{v_{bA}} = 230 - 30 = 200\,m/s$
$\therefore$ Time when the first bullet hits the car
$A,{t_1} = \frac{x}{{{v_{bA}}}} = \frac{x}{{200}}{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} \quad \quad (1)$
Distance between the car when the last bullet is fired (at time $t = {T_0} = 40\,s)$,
$x^{\prime}=x+v_{A B} \cdot T_{0}=x+10 \times 40=x+400$
Time when the last bullet hits the car $A$ is
${t_2} = {T_0} + \frac{{x'}}{{{v_{bA}}}} = 40 + \frac{{x + 400}}{{200}}{\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} {\mkern 1mu} \,\,(2)$
The interval $\Delta \,t = {t_2} - {t_1} = 40 + \frac{{x + 400}}{{200}} - \frac{x}{{200}} = 42\,s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question:
An elevator in which a man is standing is moving upwards with a speed of $5\,m{\rm{/}}s.$ If a man drops a coin from a height of $6.25\,m,$ it reaches the floor of the elevator after a time
(Take $g = 10\,m{\rm{/}}{s^2},$ $\sqrt 5 = 2.24$)
explanation:
Initially, relative velocity of coin w.r.t. lift is zero, i.e., $u = 0$
$a = + g = 10\,m{\rm{/}}{s^2},\,s = 6.25\,m$
As $s = ut + \frac{1}{2}{\rm{a}}{{\rm{t}}^2}$
$\therefore 6.25 = 0 + \frac{1}{2} \times 10\,{t^2}$
$\therefore 6.25 = 5{t^2}$
${t^2} = \frac{{6.25}}{5} = \frac{5}{4}$
$\therefore t = \frac{{\sqrt 5 }}{2} = 1.12\,s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question: A bus starts moving with acceleration $2\,m{\rm{/}}{s^2}$. A cyclist $96\,m$ behind the bus starts simultaneously towards the bus at $20\,m{\rm{/}}s$. After what time will he be able to overtake the bus?
explanation:
Let after a time $t$, the cyclist overtake the bus. Then $96 + \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times {t^2} = 20 \times t$
or ${t^2} - 20t + 96 = 0$
$\therefore t = \frac{{20 \pm \sqrt {400 - 4 \times 96} }}{{2 \times 1}}$
$ = \frac{{20 \pm 4}}{2} = 8\,{\rm{sec}}.\,\,{\rm{and}}\,\,12\,{\rm{sec}}{\rm{.}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question: Two cars, ${A}$ and ${B}$, start from ${x=0}$ and ${x=-4}$, respectively, along a straight line, and their position is given by ${x_{A}=t^{2}-3 t}$ ${x_{B}=t-4}$ At what time they cross each other?
explanation:
Crossing means same position vector at that instant for both the cars
${x_{A}=x_{B}}$
${\Rightarrow t^{2}-3 t=t-4}$
${\Rightarrow t^{2}-4 t+4=0}$
${(t-2)^{2}=0 \Rightarrow t=2 s}$

subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question:
Two trains '$A$' and '$B$' of length '$l$' and '$4 l$' are travelling into a tunnel of length '$L$' in parallel tracks from opposite directions with velocities $108\,km{\rm{/}}h$ and $72\,km{\rm{/}}h$, respectively. If train '$A$' takes $35 s$ less time than train '$B$' to cross the tunnel then, length '$L$' of tunnel is
(Given $L = 60l$)
explanation:
Speed of train $A$ and $B$
${v_A} = 108\,km{\rm{/}}h = 30\,m{\rm{/}}s$,
${v_B} = 72\,km{\rm{/}}h = 20\,m{\rm{/}}s$
Distance travelled by $A_{1}, S_{A}=L+l$
Time taken by $A, t_{A}=\dfrac{S_{A}}{v_{A}}=\dfrac{L+l}{30}$
Distance travelled $B, S_{B}=L+4 l$
Time taken by $B, t_{B}=\dfrac{S_{B}}{v_{B}}=\dfrac{L+4 l}{20}$
According to the question
$t_{B}-t_{A}=35 s$
$\Rightarrow \dfrac{L+4 l}{20}-\dfrac{L+l}{30}=35$
$\Rightarrow \dfrac{60 l+4 l}{20}-\dfrac{60 l+l}{30}=35$
$ \Rightarrow \frac{{64l}}{{20}} - \frac{{61l}}{{30}} = 35 \Rightarrow l = 30\,m$
$\therefore L = 60l = 60 \times 30 = 1800\,m$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A passenger sitting in a train $A$ moving at $90\,km{\rm{/}}h$ observes another train $B$ moving in the opposite direction for $8 s$. If the velocity of the train $B$ is $54\,km{\rm{/}}h$, then length of train $B$ is
explanation:
${v_A} = 90\,km{\rm{/}}h.$
${v_A} = 90 \times \frac{5}{{18}} = 25\,m{s^{ - 1}}$
${v_B} = 54\,km{\rm{/}}h$
${v_B} = 54 \times \frac{5}{{18}} = 15\,m{s^{ - 1}}$
Velocity of $B$ relative to train $A$ is
$\vec{V}_{B / A}=\vec{V}_{B}-\vec{V}_{A}$.
${V_{B/A}} = [15 - ( - 25)]m{\rm{/}}s = 40\,m{\rm{/}}s$
$\therefore$ Length of train
$l = {V_{B/A}} \times t = 40 \times 8\,ms$
$ = 320\,ms$.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question:
Assertion: If two particles, moving with constant velocities are to meet, the relative velocity must be along the line joining the two particles.

Reason: Relative velocity means motion of one particle as viewed from the other.
explanation:
For two particles to meet, their relative velocities must be directed along the line joining the two particles. This ensures that they are moving towards each other.
Relative velocity refers to the motion of one particle as observed from the frame of reference of the other particle. It takes into account both the magnitude and direction of motion. The reason provides a correct explanation for why the assertion is true. So correct option is (1)

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question:
Assertion: A body, whatever its motion is always at rest in a frame of reference which is fixed to the body itself.

Reason: The relative velocity of a body with respect to itself is zero.
explanation:
In the frame of reference fixed to the body itself, the body is always at rest because it is not changing its position relative to that frame of reference.
By definition, the relative velocity of an object with respect to itself is zero. This means that in its own frame of reference, the body is at rest.
The reason provides a correct explanation for why the assertion is true. So correct option is (1).

options.text: Both Assertion and Reason are correct and Reason is the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Both Assertion and Reason are correct but Reason is not the correct explanation of the Assertion.
Assertion is correct but Reason is incorrect.
Assertion is incorrect but reason is correct.

subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A rocket travelling at a speed of $200\,m{\rm{/}}s$ ejects its products of combustion at the speed of $1200\,m{\rm{/}}s$ relative to the rocket, then the speed of escaping vapours with respect to the person on the ground is:
explanation:
Relative velocity of combustion product of rocket w.r.t the motion of rocket
${\vec V_c} = + 1200\,m{\rm{/}}s\,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} {\vec V_r} = - 200\,m{\rm{/}}s$
Velocity of vapours is $V_{\mathrm{V}}$
$\vec{V}_{c}=\vec{V}_{v}-\left(\vec{V}_{r}\right)$
$1200 = {\vec V_v} - ( - 200)\,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} {\vec V_v} = 1000\,m{\rm{/}}s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A bus is moving with a speed of $10\,m{\rm{/}}s$ on the straight road. A scooterist wishes to overtake the bus in 50 seconds. If the bus is at a distance of $1\,km$ from the scooterist, with what speed should the scooterist chase the bus?
explanation:
Let the velocity of the scooter is $V_{s}$ Distance between the bus and scooter is $1\,km$
Velocity of bus $ = 10\,m{\rm{/}}s$
Relative velocity of scooter w.r.t the
bus $=V_{S}-10$
Time taken to overtake, $t=\dfrac{1000}{V_{S}-10}$
$50=\dfrac{1000}{V_{S}-10}$
$50 V_{S}-500=1000$
$50{V_S} = 1500 \Rightarrow {V_S} = 30\,m{\rm{/}}s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: The distance between two trucks moving towards each other is decreasing at the rate of $10\,m{\rm{/}}s$. If these trucks travel with same speeds in same direction the separation increases at the rate of $5\,m{\rm{/}}s$. The velocity of the trucks are:
explanation:
According to the given equation
${\rm{First}}\,\,\,{\rm{ case:}}$ Separation between the trucks decreases at the rate of $10\,m{\rm{/}}s$. Due to opposite relative motion of trucks towards each other-
$V_{1}+V_{2}=10$ (condition given)$\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 1 \right)$
${\rm{Second}}\,\,\,{\rm{ case:}}$ Seperation between the trucks increases due to the opposite relative motion of trucks away from each other-
$V_{1}-V_{2}=5$ (condition given) $\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\,\left( 2 \right)$
From equation (1) and (2), we get
$V_{1}+V_{2}=10$
$V_{1}-V_{2}=5$
$2 V_{1}=15$
${V_1} = 7.5\,m{\rm{/}}s$
By putting the value of $V_{1}$ in equation (1)
$7.5 + {V_2} = 10\,\,\,{\mkern 1mu} {\kern 1pt} {V_2} = 2.5\,m{\rm{/}}s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A train of $200\,m$ long travelling at $50\,m{\rm{/}}s$ overtakes another train $130\,m$ long travelling at $30\,m{\rm{/}}s$. The time taken by the first train to pass the second train is:
explanation:
Relative velocity of $1^{\text {st }}$ train w.r.t $2^{\text {nd }}$
$ = 50 - 30 = 20\,m{\rm{/}}s$
Total distance $=130+200=330$
Time taken $=\dfrac{330}{20}=16.5$ seconds
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains are moving with equal speed in opposite directions along two parallel railway tracks. If the wind is blowing with speed $u$ along the track so that the relative velocities of the trains with respect to the wind are in the ratio 1 $: 2$, then the speed of each train must be:
explanation:
Let the speed of each train be $x$ Relative velocities of trains are Train 1, $V_{r}=x-u$ (wind is along the direction of track)
Train 2, $V_{r}=x+u$ (wind is in opposite direction) According to the question
$\dfrac{x-u}{x+u}=\dfrac{1}{2} \Rightarrow 2 x-2 u=x+u \Rightarrow x=3 u$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: An elevator car whose floor to ceiling distance is equal to $3.8\,m$, starts ascending with constant acceleration of $2.2\,m{\rm{/}}{s^2}$, 4 second after the start a bolt begins falling from the ceiling of the car. The free fall time of the bolt is:
explanation:
Effective acceleration is
$g + a = 9.8 + 2.2 = 12\,m{\rm{/}}{s^2}$
As we know, $s=u t+\dfrac{1}{2} a_{e f f} t^{2}$, at the time of free fall $u=0$, then
$t = \sqrt {\frac{{2s}}{{g + a}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{2 \times 3.8}}{{12}}} = \sqrt {\frac{{3.8}}{6}} = \sqrt {0.633} $
$t = 0.132\,{\rm{sec}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question: A bird is tossing (flying to and fro) between two cars moving towards each other on a straight road. One car has speed of $27\,km\,{h^{ - 1}}$ while the other has the speed of $18\,km\,{h^{ - 1}}$. The bird starts moving from first car towards the other and is moving with the speed of $36\,km\,{h^{ - 1}}$ when the two cars were separated by $36\,km$. The total distance covered by the bird is:
explanation:
Total time taken by bird.
$ \Rightarrow t = \frac{D}{V} = \frac{{36}}{{(27 + 18)}} = \frac{{36}}{{45}}hr$
Distance travelled by bird
$S = Vt,\,S = 36 \times \frac{{36}}{{45}} = \frac{{1296}}{{45}} = 28.8\,km$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two particles, one with constant velocity ${\rm{50 m}}\,{{\rm{s}}^{{\rm{ - 1}}}}$ and the other with uniform acceleration ${\rm{10 m}}\,{{\rm{s}}^{{\rm{ - 2}}}}$ start moving simultaneously from the same place in the same direction. They will be at a distance of 125 $m$ from each other after
explanation:
For particle 1 moving with constant velocity, ${s_1} = {\rm{50}} \, t$
For particle 2 moving with constant acceleration, ${s_2} = \frac{1}{2}a{t^2} = \frac{1}{2} \times 10{t^2} = 5{t^2}$
when ${s_2} - {s_1} = 125$
The above equations
$5{t^2} - {\rm{50}}t = 125$
On solving, we get
$t = (5 \pm 5\sqrt 2 )\, s \Rightarrow t = 5 \left( {1 + \sqrt 2} \right)\, s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A plank is moving on ground with a velocity $v$ and a block is moving on the plank with respect to it with a velocity $u\left( {v > u} \right)$ as shown in figure What is the velocity of block with  respect to ground?
explanation:
${\overrightarrow v _b} = {\overrightarrow v _{b,p}} + {\overrightarrow v _p}$
$ = \left( { - u} \right) + v = v - u$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A bus starts moving with acceleration ${\rm{2 m/}}\,{{\rm{s}}^{\rm{2}}}$. A cyclist 96 $m$ behind the bus starts simultaneously towards the bus at 20 $m/s$. After what time will he be able to overtake the bus?
explanation:
Let after a time $t$, the cyclist overtake the bus. Then $96 + \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times {t^2} = 20 \times t$
$ \Rightarrow {\rm{ }}{t^2} - 20t + 96 = 0$
$\therefore t = \frac{{20 \pm \sqrt {400 - 4 \times 96} }}{{2 \times 1}}$
$ = \frac{{20 \pm 4}}{2} = 8$ sec and 12 sec
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A bus is moving with a velocity $10\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ on a straight road. A scooterist wishes to overtake the bus in 100 $s$. If the bus is at a distance of 1 $km$ from the scooterist, with what velocity should the scooterist chase the bus?
explanation:
Let the velocity of the scooter be $v$ $m{s^{ - 1}}$.
Then $(v - 10)100 = 1000\,{\rm{or }} \, v = 20 \, m{s^{ - 1}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A train is moving slowly on a straight track with a constant speed of $1\,m{s^{ - 1}}.$ A passenger in that train starts walking at a steady speed of $1\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ to the back of the train in the opposite direction of the motion of the train. So to an observer standing on the platform directly in front of that passenger, the velocity of the passenger appears to be
explanation: The relative velocity of the passenger w.r.to Platform is  ${v_{rel}} = 2 - 2 = 0\,m/s$.

options.text: $2\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ in the opposite direction of the train

subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains, each 50 $m$ long are travelling in opposite direction with velocity 10 $m/s$ and 15  $m/s$. The time of crossing is
explanation: ${\rm{Time}} = \frac{{{\rm{Total \, length}}}}{{{\rm{Relative \, velocity}}}} = \frac{{50 + 50}}{{10 + 15}} = \frac{{100}}{{25}} = 4\sec $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two cars are moving in the same direction with a speed of $30 kmh^{-1}$ . They are separated from each other by 5 $k$m. Third car moving in the opposite direction meets the two cars after an interval of 4 $min$. What is the speed of the third car?
explanation:
Let speed of the third car be $v \, kmh^{-1}$ Then, relative speed of third car with respect to two cars $ = {\rm{ }}\left( {v + 30} \right)\, kmh^{-1}$
Two cars are separated by 5 $km$ and third car crosses them in 4 min.
$\therefore v + 30 = \frac{{5 \times 60}}{4} = 75 \Rightarrow v = 45 \, kmh^{-1}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A man is 45 $m$ behind the bus, when the bus starts accelerating from rest with acceleration $2.5\, ms^{-2}$. With what minimum velocity should the  man start running to catch the bus $B$ in time $t = 6s$?
explanation:
For man: $S = ({V_{\min }}t)$ For bus:
$S - 45 = \frac{1}{2} \times 2.5 \times {t^2}$
$\therefore {V_{\min }}t - 45 = 1.25{t^2};$
Here $t = 6s$
${V_{\min }} \times 6 - 45 = 1.25 \times 36 \Rightarrow {V_{\min }} = \frac{{90}}{6} = 15 \, m{s^{ - 1}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: An elevator, whose floor to ceiling distance is equal to 2.7 $m$, starts ascending with constant acceleration of $1.2\,m{s^{ - 2}}.$ 2 sec after the start, a bolt begins falling from the ceiling of the car. The free fall time of the bolt is
explanation:
${S_r} = \frac{1}{2}{a_r}{t^2} \Rightarrow t = \sqrt {\frac{{2.7 \times 2}}{{11}}} = 0.7s$
${S_r} = $ relative displacement of the bolt w.r.to the elevator
${a_r} = $ relative acceleration of bolt w.r.t. the elevator.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: It takes one minute for a passenger standing on an escalator to reach the top. If the escalator does not move it takes him 3 minute to walk up. How long will it take for the passenger to arrive at the top if he walks up the moving escalator?
explanation:
Let speed of elevator be ${v_e}$
${t_1} = \frac{L}{{{v_e}}} \Rightarrow 1\min = \frac{L}{{{v_e}}}$
Let speed of person on stationary elevator be ${v_P}$ then ${t_2} = \frac{L}{{{v_P}}} \Rightarrow 3\min = \frac{L}{{{v_P}}}$
when the escalator is moving:
${t_3} = \frac{L}{{{v_e} + {v_P}}}\frac{L}{{\frac{L}{{1\min }} + \frac{L}{{3\min }}}} = \frac{3}{4}\min = 45 \, s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Car $A$ and $B$ are travelling as shown in the diagram. Find their time of meeting?
explanation:
${\bar u_{AB}} = 10\hat i - 5\hat j = 5\hat i$
${\bar a_{AB}} = 5\hat i - 5\hat i = 0$
${\bar u_{BB}} = 0,\,{\bar a_{BB}} = 0$
The relative motion is shown in the below diagram
${s_r} = {u_r}t + \frac{1}{2}{a_r}{t^2}$
$100 = 5(t) \Rightarrow t = 20 \, s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: From a lift moving upward with uniform acceleration ‘$a$’ a man throws a ball vertically upward with a velocity $v$ relative to the lift. The time after which it comes back to the man is
explanation:
We use the relative motion equation for displacement
${S_r} = {S_{{r_0}}} + {u_r}t + \frac{1}{2}{a_r}{t^2}$
${S_r}$-displacement of ball w.r.t. lift
Take the origin at the base of the lift
$ \Rightarrow {S_{{r_0}}} = 0$
(Since the body is projected from the floor itself)
${\vec S_r} = {\vec S_{bl}},{\vec u_r} = {\vec v_{bl}} = v\hat j$
${\vec a_r} = {\vec a_{bl}} = {\vec a_b} - {\vec a_l}$
${\vec a_r} = - g\hat i - a\hat j = - (a + g)\hat j$
When the ball strikes the floor again
${\vec S_r} = 0$ (relative displacement)
$0 = vt\,\hat j - \frac{1}{2}(a + g){t^2}\hat j$
$t = \frac{{2v}}{{a + g}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A ball $A$ is thrown up vertically with a speed $u$ and at the same instant another ball $B$ is released from a height $h$. At time $t$, the speed of $A$ relative to $B$ is
explanation:
At time $t$
Velocity of $A$, ${v_A} = u - gt$ upward
Velocity of $B$, ${v_B} = gt$ downward
If we assume that height $h$ is smaller than or equal to the maximum height reached by $A$, then at every instant ${v_A}\,{\rm{and}}\,{v_B}$ are in opposite direction.
$\therefore \quad {V_{AB}} = {v_A} + {v_B}$
$ = u - gt + gt$ [Speeds in opposite directions get added]
$ = u$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A man swimming downstream overcome a float at a point $M$. After travelling distance $D$ he turned back and passed the float at a distance of $D$/2 from the point $M$, then the ratio of speed of swimmer with respect to still water to the speed of the river will be
explanation:
Time taken by man to go from $M$ to $P$ and then $P$ to $N$ = time taken by float to go from $M$ to $N$.
Let $u$ is the velocity of river and $v$ is the velocity of the man w.r.t. the still water.
$\frac{D}{{v + u}} + \frac{D}{{2(v - u)}} = \frac{D}{{2u}}$
Simplify to get $\frac{v}{u} = 3$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: An express train is moving with a velocity ${v_1}$. Its driver finds another train that is moving on the same track in the same direction with velocity ${v_2}$. To escape collision, diver applies a retardation $a$ on the train, the minimum time of escaping collision will be
explanation:
As the trains are moving in the same direction, so the initial relative speed $\left( {{v_1} - {v_2}} \right)$ and by applying retardation final relative speed becomes is zero.
From $v = u - at \Rightarrow 0 = \left( {{v_1} - {v_2}} \right) - at \Rightarrow t = \frac{{{v_1} - {v_2}}}{a}.$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains separated by a distance of 100 $km$ approach each other with constant speed 10 $km/hr$. There is a fly that starts at one train and goes to the other train and keeps on going back and forth at a constant speed of 40 $km/hr$. Find the total distance that the fly travels before the two trains meet.
explanation:
This problem can be solved very easily with the help of relative velocity. As the two trains approach each other the time taken to meet each other is
${s_r} = {v_r}t$
$100 \, km = \left( {20\frac{{km}}{h}} \right)t \Rightarrow t = 5h$
So the fly travels for 5 $h$ with a constant speed of 40 $km/h$
${s_{fly}} = 40\frac{{km}}{h} \times 5h = 200 \, km$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A passenger is standing $‘d’ m$ away from a bus. The bus begins to move with constant acceleration ‘$a$’ away from him. To catch the  bus the passenger runs at a constant speed $v$ towards the bus. The minimum speed of the passenger so that he may catch the bus will be
explanation:
Assume that the passenger will catch the bus after time $t$.
When the passenger catches the bus $d + {S_1} = {S_2}$
Displacement of bus $\left( {{S_1}} \right) = \frac{1}{2}a{t^2}$
Displacement of passenger $\left( {{S_2}} \right) = ut$
$d + \frac{1}{2}a{t^2} = ut \Rightarrow t = \frac{{u \pm \sqrt {{u^2} - 2ad} }}{a}$
Passenger will catch when $t$ is real
i.e.,${u^2} \ge 2ad$
${u_{\min }} = \sqrt {2ad} $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Motion With Objects Having Accelerations

question: Two buses $A$ and $B$ starts from the same position in the same direction. Bus $A$ moves with uniform speed of 40 $m/s$ and starts from rest with uniform acceleration of $2 \,m/{s^2}.$ Bus $B$ starts at $t$ = 0 and bus $A$ starts at $t$ = $10 \,s$. After buses starts moving then the time during the journey in which bus $B$ was ahead of bus $A$ is
explanation:
In the time $10 \, s$ the displacement of the $B$ is
$\left( {\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times {{10}^2}} \right)$ = $100 \, m$
and its velocity is $20 \, m/s$. By the time $A$ starts its motion $B$ is ahead of $A$ by $100 \, m$. The time $\Delta t$ after which $A$ and $B$ meet is
${v_A}\Delta t = {v_B}\Delta t + \frac{1}{2}2\Delta {t^2} + 100 \, m$
${v_B} = 20 \, m/s,\,{v_A} = 40 \, m/s$
$\Delta {t^2} - 20\Delta t + 100 = 0$
$\Delta t = 10\,s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: An elevator, in which a man is standing, is moving upward with a constant acceleration of $2\,m/{s^2}.$ At some instant when speed of elevator is 10 $m/s$, the man drops a coin from a height of 1.5 $m$. Find the time taken by the coin to reach the floor.
explanation:
${d_{rel}} = \frac{1}{2}{a_{rel}}{t^2} \Rightarrow 1.5 = \frac{1}{2}(10 + 2){t^2}$
$1.5 = \frac{1}{2} \times 12 \times {t^2} \Rightarrow t = \frac{1}{2} \, \sec $
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two cars $A$ and $B$ are moving with same speed of 45 $km/h$ along same direction. If a third car $C$ coming from the opposite direction with speed of 36 $km/h$ meets two cars in an interval of 5 minutes, the distance of separation of two cars $A$ and $B$ should be (in $km$)
explanation:
Distance b/w the cars $A$ and $B$ remains constant. Let the distance ‘$x$’
Velocity of $C$ w.r.t. $A$ and $B$ is $V$ = 45 + 36 = 81 $km/h$
Distance $ = 81 \times \frac{5}{{60}} = 6.75 \, Km.$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains each of length 50 $m$ are running with constant speeds on parallel tracks. While moving in same direction one overtakes the other in 40 seconds and while moving in opposite direction one crosses the other in 20 seconds. The speeds of the trains will be
explanation:
We use the concept of relative motion
Let ${v_1}\,\& \,{v_2}$ are velocities of the two trains
We know that the displacement equation in relative frame is
${S_r} = {u_r}t + \frac{1}{2}{a_r}{t^2}\left( {{a_r} = 0} \right)$
${S_r} - $relative displacement,
When moving in opposite direction
$100 = \left( {{v_1} + {v_2}} \right)20\,\,\,\,\,\,\,({\rm{1}})$
When moving in same direction
$100 = \left( {{v_1} - {v_2}} \right)40\,\,\,\,\,({\rm{2}})$
${u_r} - $ relative velocity, ${a_r} - $ relative acceleration
By solving (1) & (2) we get
${v_1} = 15/4\,m/s\quad {v_2} = 5/4\,m/s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two cars $A$ and $B$ are moving as shown in figure. Find their time of meeting.
explanation:
Method – 1
When the two cars meet each other
$\left| {{S_A}} \right| + \left| {{S_B}} \right| = 75$
$10 \, t + 5 \, t = 75$
$t = 5 \, \sec$.
Method-2
(Relative frame method)
We can solve with respect to any car, we do it with respect to car $B$
${\overline v _{_{AB}}} = {\overline v _{_A}} - {\overline v _{_B}} = 10\hat i - \left( { - 5\hat i} \right) = 15\hat i$
Here car $B$ is at rest and car $A$ is moving with $15 \, m/s$ w.r.t. $B$
We can transfer the picture into relative frame as shown in the figure
${s_r} = {u_r}t + \frac{1}{2}{a_r}{t^2}({a_r} = 0)$
$75 = 15t \Rightarrow t = 5\sec .$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A bird flies to and fro between two cars which move with velocities ${v_1} = 20\,\,m/s\,{\rm{and}}\,{v_2} = 30\,\,m/s$. If the speed of the bird is ${v_3} = 10\,m/s$ and the initial distance of separation between them is 2 $km$, find the total distance covered by the bird till the cars meet.
explanation:
Time to meet the cars: $t = \frac{d}{{{v_1} + {v_2}}}$
Distance travelled by bird in this time
$s = {v_3}t = \frac{{{v_3}d}}{{{v_1} + {v_2}}} = \frac{{10 \times 2000}}{{\left( {20 + 30} \right)}} = 400 \, m$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two trains $A$ and $B$ of length 400 $m$ each are moving on two parallel tracks with a uniform speed of 72 $km/h$ in the same direction, with $A$ ahead of $B$. The driver of $B$ decides to overtake $A$ and acceleration by $1m/{s^2}.$ If after 50 $s$, the ground of $B$ just brushes past the driver of $A$, what was the original distance between them?
explanation:
Length of each train,
$A = B = 400 \, m$
Initial velocities of both trains,
${v_A} = {v_B} = 42 \, km/h = 20\,m/s$
Distance travelled by train $A$ in 50 $s$
${S_A} = {v_A} \times t$
${S_A} = 20 \times 50 = 1000\,m$
Distance travelled by train $B$ in 50 $s$,
${S_B} = {u_B}t + \frac{1}{2}{a_g}{t^2}$
${S_B} = 20 \times 50 + \frac{1}{2} \times 1 \times {\left( {50} \right)^2}$
= 1000 + 1250 = 2250 $m$
Original distance between the two trains
$ = {S_B} - {S_A}$
= 2250 - 1000 = 1250 $m$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A jet airplane travelling at a speed of 500 $km/h$ ejects its products of combustion at the speed of 1500 $km/h$ relative to the jet plane. What is  the velocity of the latter with respect to an observer on the ground?
explanation:
Let jet airplane be moving upwards right (+ve direction) with velocity ${v_J}$ and ejected gases be moving downwards (-ve direction) with velocity ${v_g}$ while observer be at rest on the ground i.e.,${v_0} = 0$
$\therefore {v_J} = 500 \, km/h$
${v_{gJ}} = - 1500 \, km/h \Rightarrow {v_g} - {v_J} = - 1500\,kmph$
$\therefore {v_g} = - 1500 + 500 = - 1000\,km{h^{ - 1}}$
Therefore, relative velocity of the ejected gases with respect to the observer is $1000 \, km/h$, in downward direction.
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A police van is moving on a highway with a speed of 30 $km/h$ fires a bullet at a thiefs car speeding away in the same direction with a speed of 192 $km/h$. If the muzzle speed of the bullet is 150 $m/s$, with what speed does the bullet hit the thief’s car?
explanation:
Speed of police van
$\left( {{v_p}} \right) = 30km/h = \frac{{25}}{3}m/s$ $\left( {\because 1\,km/s = \frac{5}{{18}}m/s} \right)$
Speed of thief’s car $({v_\gamma }) = 192\,km/h = \frac{{160}}{3}m/s.$
Muzzle speed of bullet $({v_B}) = 150\,m/s$
The effective speed of the bullet
${v_B} = {v_B} + {v_p}$
$ = 150 + \frac{{25}}{3} = \frac{{475}}{3}m/s$
Speed of the bullet with which it hits the thief’s car
$ = \left( {\frac{{475}}{3} - \frac{{160}}{3}} \right)m/s = 105 \, m/s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: On a two lane road, car $A$ is travelling with a speed of 36 $km/h$. Two cars $B$ and $C$ approach car $A$ in opposite directions with a speed of 54 $km/h$ each. At a certain instant, when the distance AB is equal to AC, both being 1 $km$, $B$ decides to overtake $A$ before $C$ does. In this case, the acceleration of car $B$ is required to avoid an accident
explanation:
Speed of car $A\left( {{v_A}} \right) = 36\,km/h = 10\,m/s$
Speed of car $B$ and car $C$
${u_A} = {u_C} = 54 \, km/h = 15\,m/s$
Relative velocity of car $B$ w.r.t., car $A$
${u_{BA}} = {u_B} - {u_A} = 15 - 10 = 5 \, m/s$
Relative velocity of car $C$ w.r.t., car $A$
${u_{CA}} = {u_C} - {u_A} = 15 - \left( { - 10} \right) = 25 \, m/s$
Distance between car $A$ and car $B$ = $1 \, km = 1000 \, m$
Time taken by car $C$ to travel distance $AC = 1000 \, m$
$ = \frac{{1000}}{{25}} = 40 \, s$
Let car $B$ starts to accelerate with an acceleration $a$, using equation of motion.
$s = ut + \frac{1}{2}a{t^2}$
$s = {u_{BA}}t + \frac{1}{2}a{t^2}$
$1000 = 5 \times 40 + \frac{1}{2}a \times {\left( {40} \right)^2}$
$ \Rightarrow a = 1m/{s^2}$
Therefore, car $B$ should accelerate with an acceleration $1 \, m/{s^2}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Preeti reached the metro station and found that the escalator was not working. She walked up the stationary escalator in time ${t_1}.$ On other days, if she remains stationary on the moving escalator, then the escalator takes her up in time ${t_2}.$ The time taken by her to walk up on the moving escalator will be
explanation:
$v_1 = $ velocity of Preeti
$v_2 = $ velocity of escalator
$\ell = $ distance
$t = \frac{\ell }{{{{\rm{v}}_1} + {{\rm{v}}_2}}} = \frac{\ell }{{\frac{\ell }{{{t_1}}} + \frac{\ell }{{{t_2}}}}} = \frac{{{t_1}{t_2}}}{{{t_1} + {t_2}}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A bus is moving with a velocity $10\,m{s^{ - 1}}$ on a straight road. A scooterist wishes to overtake the bus in $100 \, s$. If the bus is at a distance of 1 $km$ from the scooterist, with what velocity should the scooterist chase the bus?
explanation:
Let the velocity of the scooter be $v\,m{s^{ - 1}}$.
Then $(v - 10)100 = 1000\,{\rm{or }}v = 20 \, m{s^{ - 1}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: A motorboat covers a given distance in 6 hours moving downstream on a river. It covers the  same distance in 10 hours moving upstream. The time it taken to cover the same distance in still water is
explanation:
Let ${v_w}$ be the velocity of water and ${v_b}$ be the velocity of motorboat in still water.
The distance $x$ covered by the motorboat in moving downstream in 6 $h$ is
$x = ({v_b} + {v_w}) \times 6\,\,\,\,\,\,(1)$
Same distance $x$ covered by the motorboat in moving upstream in 10 $h$ is
$x = ({v_b} - {v_w}) \times 10\,\,\,\,\,\,(2)$
Equating (1) and (2), we get
$({v_b} + {v_w}) \times 6 = ({v_b} - {v_w}) \times 10$
${v_w} = \frac{{{v_b}}}{4}$
$\therefore \,x = ({v_b} + {v_w}) \times 6 = 7.5\,\,{v_b}$
Time taken by the motorboat to cover the same distance in still water is
$t = \frac{x}{{{v_b}}} = \frac{{7.5{v_b}}}{{{v_b}}} = 7.5\,{\rm{hours}}$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: At a metro station, a girl walks up a stationary escalator in $20 \, sec$. If she remains stationary on the escalator, then the escalator take her up in $30 \, sec$. The time taken by her to walk up on the moving escalator will be
explanation:
Let $x$ be the length of the escalator
Speed of the girl on stationary escalator
${v_g} = \frac{x}{{20}}$
Speed of the escalator ${v_e} = \frac{x}{{30}}$
When both the girl and the escalator move up,
or $t = \frac{x}{{{v_g} + {v_e}}}$
$t = \frac{x}{{\frac{x}{{20}} + \frac{x}{{30}}}}$
$\therefore $ Time taken, $t$ = $12 \, s$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities

question: Two bodies $A$ and $B$ start from rest from the same point with a uniform acceleration of $2 \, m{s^2}$. If $B$ starts one second later then the bodies are separated, at the end of the next second, by
explanation:
${S_B} = \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times {\left( 1 \right)^2} = 1\,m$
${S_A} = \frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times {\left( 2 \right)^2} = 4\,m$
$\therefore \quad {S_A} - {S_B} = 3\,m$
subject: Physics
subtopic: Relative Displacement/ Relative motion with objects having velocities
